join_order.notes: TPC-H c_custkey is 1-based sequential [1..1500000]; build a bitset of size 1500001 indexed directly by custkey value (~188 KB, fits in L3). Probe with o_custkey: O(1) array lookup, no hashing.

join_order.build: orders(filtered: o_orderdate < 8015 AND o_custkey in customer_bitset)
join_order.notes: Build compact open-addressing hash map: key=o_orderkey (int32), value=packed struct {int32 o_orderdate, int32 o_shippriority}. Capacity 4194304 (next power-of-2 above 1.47M/0.5). Total ~50 MB — fits in memory, partially in L3 (44 MB). Use same hash32 function. Probe from lineitem morsel workers.

filters.index_note: Break loop early when zone_min[b] >= 8015 (sorted ascending). ~74 of 150 blocks fully included; ~76 trailing blocks fully skipped.
Skip blocks where zone_max[b] <= 8015 (continue). ~294 of 600 leading blocks entirely skipped. For blocks where zone_min[b] > 8015, skip per-row shipdate check.

data_structures.customer_filter: direct_array bitset — bool qualifying_cust[1500001], indexed by c_custkey value; ~1.5 MB; fully cache-resident
data_structures.orders_build_map: compact_hash_map(pre_sized, capacity=4194304) — open-addressing linear probing; key=int32 o_orderkey (sentinel=-1), value=packed int64 encoding {int32 o_orderdate | int32 o_shippriority}; ~50 MB total
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local unordered flat hash maps keyed by int32 l_orderkey -> {double revenue, int32 o_orderdate, int32 o_shippriority}; pre-sized per-thread to ~3M/nthreads entries; merged into single map after scan

parallelism.phase_1_customer_scan: single-threaded (1.5M rows x 1 byte = 1.5 MB — trivially fast, avoids bitset write-contention)
parallelism.phase_2_orders_scan: single-threaded (build map serially; 15M orders, ~50% skipped by zone map; avoids concurrent hash-map insertion complexity)
parallelism.phase_3_lineitem_scan: parallel morsel-driven across all 64 threads; each thread owns a local aggregation hash map; no shared state during scan
parallelism.phase_4_merge: linear merge of 64 thread-local maps into final map; single-threaded or tree-reduce

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — break early once zone_min[b] >= kOrderDateMax (sorted order)
skip_blocks — skip leading ~294/600 blocks where zone_max[b] <= kShipdateMin; omit per-row check for blocks where zone_min[b] > kShipdateMin

aggregation: hash_group_by — key=l_orderkey (int32, uniquely identifies group since each order has exactly one o_orderdate and o_shippriority); value={double revenue, int32 o_orderdate, int32 o_shippriority}; estimated ~3M groups total

output.strategy: partial_sort or min-heap of 10 over final merged map

memory_budget.aggregation_maps_MB: ~64 x (3M/64 groups x 20 bytes) = ~60 MB total across threads
